executiveSummary.summary: Strong founder-market fit with your fashion industry background, but facing significant marketplace chicken-and-egg challenges. The sustainability angle provides differentiation, but customer acquisition costs and brand margin pressure are legitimate concerns. Success hinges on solving the cold-start problem with a focused brand cohort.

marketSize.sam: $2.1B US sustainable fashion e-commerce (estimated 14% of total sustainable fashion)
marketSize.som: $5-10M realistic 3-year GMV target (capturing 0.2-0.5% of US market)
marketSize.methodology: Based on sustainable fashion growth rates (15-20% annually) and typical marketplace penetration patterns

competitors.name: The Citizenry
competitors.description: Curated sustainable home and fashion marketplace
competitors.weakness: Limited fashion focus, higher price points

competitors.name: Reformation
competitors.description: Direct-to-consumer sustainable fashion brand with marketplace features
competitors.strength: Strong brand identity and customer loyalty
competitors.weakness: Limited third-party brands, focused on women's fashion

competitors.name: Etsy
competitors.description: Handmade and vintage marketplace with sustainability filters

competitors.name: ASOS Marketplace
competitors.description: Fashion marketplace with some sustainable brands

marketAnalysis.positioning: The trusted curator for verified sustainable fashion brands - solving discovery and trust for conscious consumers
marketAnalysis.timing: Strong tailwinds from Gen Z/Millennial values shift and post-pandemic consciousness about consumption
gaps: Lack of trusted curation in sustainable fashion
No platform focused exclusively on verified sustainability credentials
Fragmented brand discovery for conscious consumers

valueProposition.coreProblem: Conscious consumers can't easily discover and trust sustainable fashion brands, while sustainable brands struggle with expensive customer acquisition
valueProposition.targetCustomer: Women aged 25-45, household income $75K+, already shopping sustainable brands but frustrated by discovery process
valueProposition.uniqueAngle: Strict sustainability verification combined with fashion industry curation expertise - quality over quantity
valueProposition.whyNow: Sustainability washing backlash creates demand for verified platforms, plus supply chain transparency tools now make verification scalable

businessModel.revenueStrategy: Transaction fees (15%) plus subscription revenue ($99/mo) creating dual revenue streams
businessModel.pricingFramework: 15% commission competitive with Etsy (6.5%) but justified by curation value; $99/mo covers verification costs

unitEconomics.estimatedCAC: $45-65 based on sustainable fashion CPCs and conversion rates

businessModel.scalabilityAssessment: Revenue scales with GMV, but curation costs scale with brand count. Need to build verification systems and processes early.

technicalFeasibility.recommendedStack: Shopify Plus for marketplace infrastructure, custom verification portal

buildVsBuy.rationale: Shopify Plus handles payments, inventory, shipping - focus resources on differentiation
Core differentiator, needs custom criteria and workflow

risks.description: Customer acquisition costs in fashion e-commerce average $50-80, potentially making unit economics challenging
risks.mitigation: Focus on organic growth through brand partnerships and content marketing, leverage fashion industry connections

risks.description: Existing marketplaces could add sustainability filters and verification
risks.mitigation: Build deep brand relationships and proprietary verification processes that create switching costs

risks.description: 15% commission may be too high for sustainable brands already operating on thin margins
risks.mitigation: Test pricing with initial brand cohort, consider tiered commission structure based on volume

risks.description: Marketplace chicken-and-egg problem: need brands to attract consumers and consumers to attract brands
risks.mitigation: Launch with small, committed brand cohort and their existing customer bases

week1to4: Interview 20 sustainable fashion brand founders about marketplace pain points and commission sensitivity
Survey 50 conscious consumers about discovery challenges
Research and document sustainability verification criteria
Analyze 5 competitor pricing and commission structures
week5to8: Recruit 8-12 committed launch brands through your network
Build MVP verification system and brand onboarding
week9to12: Soft launch with beta brands and their customer lists
Gather feedback and iterate on UX

decisionAgenda.question: Build custom platform vs use Shopify marketplace tools?

options.tradeoff: Full control and differentiation vs 6+ months development time and higher costs
Faster launch and lower costs vs limited customization and ongoing fees

decisionAgenda.recommendation: Start with Shopify Plus. Your differentiation is curation, not technology. You can always migrate later with proven traction.

decisionAgenda.question: Launch with 10 brands or 100?

options.tradeoff: Better relationships and support vs limited selection for consumers
More selection and GMV potential vs diluted curation and support

decisionAgenda.recommendation: Launch with 8-12 brands maximum. Focus on brands with existing customer bases who can drive initial traffic. Quality over quantity is your positioning.

decisionAgenda.question: Commission rate: 15% or lower?

options.tradeoff: Higher revenue per transaction vs potential brand resistance
More brand-friendly vs lower revenue from smaller brands

decisionAgenda.recommendation: Test 15% with launch brands but prepare 10% tier for high-volume brands. Use your fashion connections to validate pricing before launch.
